drugs.name: Aspirin
drugs.description: Used to reduce pain, fever, or inflammation and as a blood thinner.

drugs.name: Warfarin
drugs.description: Blood thinner used to prevent blood clots.

drugs.name: Ibuprofen
drugs.description: Used for pain relief and reducing inflammation.

drugs.name: Lisinopril
drugs.description: Used to treat high blood pressure and heart failure.

drugs.name: Simvastatin
drugs.description: Used to lower cholesterol and prevent cardiovascular disease.

drugs.name: Metformin
drugs.description: First-line medication for the treatment of type 2 diabetes.

drugs.name: Spironolactone
drugs.description: Used to treat fluid build-up and high blood pressure.

drugs.name: Digoxin
drugs.description: Used to treat heart failure and certain heart rhythm problems.

interactions.description: Increased risk of major bleeding. Monitor INR closely.
Significantly increases bleeding risk. Avoid if possible.
interactions.mechanism: Pharmacodynamic synergism and GI mucosal injury.

interactions.description: NSAID duplication. Ibuprofen may also interfere with Aspirin's antiplatelet effect.
Spironolactone may decrease renal clearance of Digoxin.
Simvastatin may slightly increase the anticoagulant effect of Warfarin.
interactions.mechanism: Inhibition of CYP3A4-mediated metabolism of Warfarin (minor).
